description_long: This object is a slender, leaf-shaped blade with a pointed tip and a slightly curved, symmetrical profile. The surface is textured with visible flaking patterns, suggesting it is knapped from a single piece of stone, likely flint or chert. The edges are finely serrated, indicating it was crafted for cutting or piercing. There is no visible handle, guard, or decorative elements, and the entire object appears to be a single material throughout. The coloration is a pale beige with subtle variations, typical of natural stone. Its size and form suggest it could be an ancient or primitive knife or small spear point, suitable for hand-held use.
description: A slender, leaf-shaped stone blade with serrated edges and a pointed tip, made from pale beige flint or chert. The surface is textured with flaking patterns and lacks a handle or ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full leaf-shaped blade, pointed tip, and visible flaking patterns.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin, flat profile and slight curvature of the blade.
description_view_2: Back view displays the serrated edges and overall symmetry of the blade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the blade's thinness and consistent width.
attribute_handle_design: no distinct handle, integral stone body
